title: NDA General Ability 10 April 2022
question: Which one of the following statements with respect to Global Positioning System (GPS) is not correct?
options: It is based on network of satellites orbiting above the Earth.
It is based on the system of triangulation.
GPS receivers provide location in terms of latitude, longitude and altitude.
It provides information exclusively for military operations.
solution:
The statement with respect to Global Positioning System (GPS) that is not correct is:

Option D: It provides information exclusively for military operations.

Explanation:

Option A: It is based on a network of satellites orbiting above the Earth.

This statement is correct. GPS relies on a constellation of satellites that orbit the Earth and transmit signals. GPS receivers use these signals to determine their position.

Option B: It is based on the system of triangulation.

This statement is slightly misleading in its wording. While GPS positioning is often referred to using the concept of triangulation, it actually uses a method called trilateration. Trilateration involves measuring distances from multiple satellites (not angles which is more typical for triangulation) to pinpoint a location.

Option C: GPS receivers provide location in terms of latitude, longitude, and altitude.

This statement is correct. GPS receivers can precisely calculate a location's latitude, longitude, and altitude (elevation) using signals from multiple satellites.

Option D: It provides information exclusively for military operations.

This statement is incorrect. While GPS was originally developed by the U.S. Department of Defense and was initially intended for military purposes, it is now widely used for civilian applications as well. GPS technology is utilized in various fields including navigation, agriculture, surveying, time synchronization, and more.

To summarize, the correct answer is Option D.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: Which one of the following statements is correct ?
options: A neutron is formed by combination of an electron and a proton. Therefore, it is neutral
The mass of an electron is about 1/2000 times that of a proton
An isotope of cobalt is used in the treatment of goiter
J. J. Thomson proposed that the nucleus of an atom contains only neutrons
solution:
Option 2 is correct.

Explanation:

The mass of an electron is

$$m_e \approx 9.11\times10^{-31}\,\text{kg},$$

while that of a proton is

$$m_p \approx 1.67\times10^{-27}\,\text{kg}.$$

Their ratio is

$$\frac{m_e}{m_p}\approx\frac{1}{1836}\approx\frac{1}{2000}.$$

Why the others are wrong:

Option 1 describes an outdated idea; neutrons aren’t simply proton+electron combinations.

Option 3 mixes up treatments: radioiodine isotopes help treat goiter, not cobalt.

Option 4 is incorrect—J. J. Thomson’s model didn’t place neutrons in the nucleus (neutrons were discovered later by Chadwick).

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: Which one of the following particles in the nucleus of an atom was discovered by J. Chadwick?
solution:
Looking at the history of particle discoveries:

The electron was discovered by J.J. Thomson.

The proton was identified by Ernest Rutherford.

The positron was discovered by Carl Anderson.

The neutron was discovered by James Chadwick.

And among the particles listed, the electron is found outside the nucleus, while protons and neutrons are found inside the nucleus. The positron is an antiparticle and not a standard part of the nucleus.

So, the particle in the nucleus discovered by J. Chadwick is the Neutron. That's option 4!

Chadwick's discovery in 1932 was super important for understanding the atom. He found this neutral particle that had a mass very close to that of a proton, which helped explain the mass of atoms that couldn't just be accounted for by protons alone.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: An object is placed between infinity and the pole (P) of a convex mirror. The position of the image is
options: between pole (P) and the focus (F), behind the mirror
between the focus (F) and infinity, behind the mirror
between the pole (P) and the infinity, in front of the mirror
at the focus (F), behind the mirror
solution:
For a convex mirror the mirror‐formula

$$\frac1u + \frac1v = \frac1f$$

applies, with

$u>0$ (object in front of mirror),

$f<0$ (convex mirror focal length),

$v<0$ (image behind mirror).

If $u\to\infty$, then $1/v=1/f$ ⇒ $v=f$ (image at the focus, behind).

As $u$ decreases from ∞ toward 0, $\;1/v=1/f-1/u$ becomes more negative ⇒ $|v|$ decreases from $|f|$ toward 0.

So for any finite object distance the image lies between the pole $P$ and the focus $F$, behind the mirror.

Answer: Option 1.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: The work done in moving a charge of 2 coulomb (C) from point A to point B is 24 J. What is the potential difference between A and B?
solution:
The potential difference between A and B is given by

$$ \Delta V \;=\;\frac{W}{q} $$

where

• $W = 24\text{ J}$ is the work done, and

• $q = 2\text{ C}$ is the charge.

Plugging in,

$$ \Delta V = \frac{24}{2} = 12\text{ V}. $$

So the correct choice is 12 V.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: Two conducting wires of the same material and of equal lengths and equal diameters are first connected in parallel and then in series in a circuit across the same potential difference. The ratio of heat produced in parallel and series combinations is
solution:
Let the resistance of each wire be $R$.

Since both wires are identical:

  • In series, equivalent resistance:

$$ R_s = R + R = 2R $$

  • In parallel, equivalent resistance:

$$ R_p = \frac{R \cdot R}{R + R} = \frac{R}{2} $$

Now, heat produced in a circuit is given by Joule’s law:

$$ H = \frac{V^2 t}{R} $$

(where $V$ and $t$ are same in both cases)

So,

For parallel combination:

$$ H_p = \frac{V^2 t}{R_p} = \frac{V^2 t}{R/2} = \frac{2V^2 t}{R} $$

For series combination:

$$ H_s = \frac{V^2 t}{R_s} = \frac{V^2 t}{2R} $$

Now, ratio:

$$ \frac{H_p}{H_s} = \frac{2V^2 t / R}{V^2 t / (2R)} = 4 $$

So, the ratio is:

$$ 4:1 $$

Correct option: Option 2

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: The magnetic field inside a long straight solenoid-carrying current
options: decreases as we move towards its end
increases as we move towards its end
solution:
The magnetic field in an ideal, infinitely long, tightly‐wound solenoid is the same everywhere inside. In fact, by Ampère’s law one finds

$$B \,=\, \mu_0\,n\,I$$

where

• μ₀ is the permeability of free space,

• n is the number of turns per unit length, and

• I is the current through the winding.

Because this expression has no dependence on the position along the axis, the field is uniform inside. (Near the very ends of a real, finite solenoid the field does fall off, but for a “long” solenoid those edge effects are negligible.)

Answer: Option 4.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: A car has an initial velocity of 12 m/s and is brought to rest over a distance of 45 m. The acceleration of the car is
solution:
We can use the kinematic relation

$$v^2 = u^2 + 2as$$

where

• $u = 12$ m/s (initial speed)

• $v = 0$ m/s (final speed)

• $s = 45$ m (distance)

• $a$ is the (constant) acceleration

Plugging in:

$$0^2 = 12^2 + 2\,a\,(45)$$

$$0 = 144 + 90\,a$$

$$a = -\frac{144}{90} = -1.6\text{ m/s}^2$$

So the acceleration is $-1.6$ m/s² (Option 3).

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: The universal constant of gravitation G has the unit
solution:
The correct choice is Option 4: N·m²/kg².

From Newton’s law of universal gravitation,

$$F = G \frac{m_1\,m_2}{r^2}$$

Solving for G gives

$$G = \frac{F\,r^2}{m_1\,m_2}$$

– Force F has units of newtons (N)

– Distance r has units of meters (m)

– Mass m₁, m₂ have units of kilograms (kg)

So

$$[G] = \frac{\text{N}\times\text{m}^2}{\text{kg}^2}.$$

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: Two bodies of unequal masses are dropped from a tower. At any instant, they have equal
solution:
Concept Explanation:

When two objects are in free fall, ignoring air resistance, gravity is the primary force affecting their motion. Here are the key points to consider:

Acceleration: Every object in free fall experiences the same acceleration due to gravity, which is approximately 9.8 m/s², regardless of their mass.

Momentum: Momentum is defined as the product of mass and velocity (momentum = mass × velocity). Since the two bodies have different masses, their momenta will not be equal.

Kinetic Energy: This is calculated using the formula KE = (1/2) × mass × velocity². Given that the masses differ, the kinetic energies of the two bodies will also be different.

Potential Energy: The potential energy formula is PE = mass × g × height. Different masses equate to different potential energies, even if the height is the same.

Therefore, the only physical quantity that remains equal for both bodies is acceleration.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: The length of a simple pendulum is increased four times to its previous value while the mass is doubled. What is the ratio of the new and previous time period of the pendulum?
solution:
Calculation:

The time period of a simple pendulum is given by the formula:

$ T_{\text{old}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

If the length $ L $ of the pendulum is increased four times and the mass is doubled, we note that the time period is independent of the pendulum's mass. Therefore, the time period depends only on the length and gravitational acceleration.

For the new pendulum:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{4L}{g}} $

Simplifying this expression:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2\pi\sqrt{4}\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2 \times 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

Thus, comparing the initial and new time periods:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2T_{\text{old}} $

The ratio of the new to the old time period is:

$ T_{\text{new}} : T_{\text{old}} = 2 : 1 $

Therefore, the ratio of the new and previous time periods is 2:1.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: Which one of the following is NOT a basic property of electric charge?
options: Charge on a body is always an integral multiple of an electron or a proton charge
Charges can be created and destroyed in an isolated system
solution:
The statement that is NOT a basic property of electric charge is

Option 4: “Charges can be created and destroyed in an isolated system.”

Here’s why the others are basic properties:

Charges can be added: electric charges obey superposition, so net charge is the algebraic sum of individual charges.

Charge is conserved: in any process, total charge remains constant.

Charge quantization: any observable charge is an integer multiple of the elementary charge $e$.

By contrast, creation or destruction of net charge in isolation would violate charge conservation.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: What happens when the sunlight travels through the Earth’s atmosphere?
options: The blue colour is scattered more compared to the red colour
The red colour is scattered more compared to the blue colour
Both the blue and the red colours are scattered equally
The blue colour is not scattered but the red colour is scattered the most
solution:
Concept:

Scattering of Light: This is a phenomenon where light rays are redirected in various directions when they encounter particles of a size similar to the wavelength of the light.

Rayleigh Scattering: When light scatters off molecules in the air or particles up to about a tenth of the wavelength of the light, it is known as Rayleigh scattering.

According to Rayleigh scattering:

$ \text{Scattering} \propto \frac{1}{{\lambda^4}} $

Where $\lambda$ is the wavelength of light.

For white light, the order of wavelength is:

Violet < Indigo < Blue < Green < Yellow < Orange < Red

Explanation:

Scattering is inversely proportional to the fourth power of the wavelength of light.

Among primary colors (blue, green, yellow, orange, and red), blue light has the shortest wavelength and is therefore scattered the most.

The scattered light in the sky is rich in blue light, which is why the sky appears blue. Hence, the correct conclusion aligns with this explanation.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question:
When a solid body is partially or completely immersed in a fluid, the fluid exerts an upward force on the body. The magnitude of the force is equal to
1.the mass of the body
2.the weight of the displaced fluid by the body
Which of the above is/are correct?
solution:
The correct choice is 2 only.

By Archimedes’ principle, the buoyant force equals the weight of the fluid displaced:

$$F_{\rm b} = \rho_{\rm fluid}\,V_{\rm disp}\,g$$

where

• ρ₍fluid₎ is the fluid’s density

• V₍disp₎ is the volume of fluid displaced

• g is the acceleration due to gravity.

It is not equal to the mass of the body.

question: If the length of a copper wire is increased by twice, then its resistivity will be
solution:
The correct answer is Option 3: Same.

• Resistivity (ρ) is an intrinsic property of the material—it doesn’t depend on the wire’s length or cross-section.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: Which one of the following is dimensionless quantity?
solution:
Strain is the only dimensionless quantity among the four.

• Stress has units of force per area:

$$[{\rm Stress}] = \frac{F}{A} = \frac{MLT^{-2}}{L^2} = M\,L^{-1}\,T^{-2}.$$

• Pressure is the same dimensionally as stress:

$$[{\rm Pressure}] = M\,L^{-1}\,T^{-2}.$$

• Force has units:

$$[F] = M\,L\,T^{-2}.$$

• Strain is a ratio of lengths:

$$\text{Strain} = \frac{\Delta L}{L}$$

so all the length dimensions cancel, making it dimensionless.

Answer: Option 2 (Strain).

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: A system that does NOT allow exchange of heat with its surrounding is called
solution:
Explanation:

Adiabatic Process:

An adiabatic process occurs in a thermodynamic system when there is no exchange of heat between the system and its surroundings. This means that throughout the adiabatic process, the system insulates itself against heat transfer.

Mathematically, this is represented by $ ΔQ = 0 $, indicating no heat transfer.

Additional Information:

Isothermal Process: In this process, the system undergoes changes such that its temperature remains constant.

Isobaric Process: This involves changes where the pressure remains constant.

Isochoric Process: Here, the system's volume remains constant during the change.

Isentropic Process:

In an isentropic process, the entropy of the system remains constant. It is also referred to as a constant-entropy process.

This process is both reversible and adiabatic, meaning no heat or matter is transferred.

It is an idealized concept in thermodynamics, often serving as a model to aspire to.

Entropy:

Entropy measures the amount of thermal energy in a system that cannot be used to perform work, per unit of temperature.

Over time, the entropy of the world tends to increase as systems move towards less equilibrium.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: If the speed of light in carbon disulfide and vacuum is XXXXX X X and YYYYY Y Y respectively, then
options: X < Y
X > Y
X ≥ Y
X = Y
solution:
Light travels slower in a medium than in vacuum because its refractive index $n>1$. For carbon disulfide ($n_{\rm CS_2}\approx1.63$) we have

speed in CS₂: $X = \dfrac{c}{n_{\rm CS_2}}$

speed in vacuum: $Y = c$

Since $n_{\rm CS_2}>1$,

$$X = \frac{c}{n_{\rm CS_2}} < c = Y\,. $$

Answer: Option 1, $X < Y$.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: A current through a horizontal power line flows in east to west direction. What will be the direction of magnetic field at a point directly below it when viewed from east end?
options: Clockwise in a plane perpendicular to the wire
Anti-clockwise in a plane perpendicular to the wire
Clockwise in a plane of parallel to the wire
Anti-clockwise in a plane of parallel to the wire
solution:
CONCEPT:

Right-hand thumb rule:

According to this rule, if you imagine holding the linear wire conductor in the grip of your right hand so that your thumb points in the direction of the current, the curvature of your fingers around the conductor will represent the direction of the magnetic field lines.

EXPLANATION:

If the current through a horizontal power line flows from east to west, the direction of the magnetic field at a point directly below it will be from north to south. Therefore, the correct option is anti-clockwise in a plane perpendicular to the wire.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: When a light ray passes through from air to water with a non-zero angle the ray will be
solution:
When light goes from air (n₁≈1.00) into water (n₂≈1.33) at some angle θ₁ to the normal, Snell’s law tells us:

$$n_1\sin\theta_1 \;=\; n_2\sin\theta_2\,. $$

Because n₂>n₁, we have sin θ₂<sin θ₁ ⇒ θ₂<θ₁.

That means the refracted ray bends toward the normal.

Answer: Option 1 – bending towards the normal.

question: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?

solution:

question: Which one of the following is a non-conventional source of energy?
solution:
Non-conventional (renewable or alternative) sources are things like solar, wind, tidal, geothermal, etc.

• Petroleum, natural gas and coal are all conventional (fossil) fuels.

• Tidal energy harnesses the rise and fall of ocean tides, making it a renewable—hence non-conventional—source.

Answer: Option 3 (Tidal energy).

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: If three resistors of 1 Ohm each connect in parallel to each other the resultant resistance is
solution:
For resistors in parallel, the reciprocal of the total resistance is the sum of reciprocals:

$$ \frac1{R_{\rm eq}} = \frac1{R_1} + \frac1{R_2} + \frac1{R_3} = \frac1{1} + \frac1{1} + \frac1{1} = 3 $$

So

$$ R_{\rm eq} = \frac1{3}\,\Omega. $$

That corresponds to Option 2: 1/3 Ohm.

title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
question: We hear an echo due to
solution:
Concept:

Echo: An echo occurs when a sound wave reflects off a surface and is heard a short time after the original sound. This delay is caused by the time it takes for the sound wave to travel to the surface and back.

Reflection of sound: When sound waves encounter a hard surface, they bounce back, producing a reflected sound wave.

Conditions for Echo Formation:

The distance between the sound source and the reflecting surface should be at least 17.2 meters, assuming the speed of sound is approximately 344 m/s.

The reflecting surface should be large and hard to effectively reflect the sound.

Therefore, the phenomenon of hearing an echo is explained by the reflection of sound waves.

solution:

$$a = \frac{dv}{dt} = 0\,. $$

question: The focal length of a concave lens is 0.5 m. The power of the lens is
solution:
For a lens, power is given by

$$ P = \frac{1}{f} $$

where $f$ is in metre.

Since it is a concave lens, its focal length is negative:

$$ f = -0.5 \, \text{m} $$

Now,

$$ P = \frac{1}{-0.5} = -2 \, \text{D} $$

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{-2.0 \, \text{D}} $$

Option 4

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: The rule to determine the direction of a force experienced by a straight current carrying conductor placed in a magnetic field which is perpendicular to it is
solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 2: Fleming’s left-hand rule

Explanation:

When a straight current-carrying conductor is placed in a magnetic field perpendicular to it, it experiences a force.

To find the direction of this force, we use Fleming’s left-hand rule.

Fleming’s left-hand rule:

Stretch the thumb, forefinger, and middle finger of your left hand so that they are mutually perpendicular.

Then:

  • Forefinger shows the direction of magnetic field

  • Middle finger shows the direction of current

  • Thumb shows the direction of force or motion

Why other options are wrong:

  • Right-hand thumb rule: Used to find the direction of magnetic field around a current-carrying conductor.

  • Fleming’s right-hand rule: Used for direction of induced current in electromagnetic induction.

  • Hund’s rule: Related to filling of electrons in atomic orbitals.

So, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Fleming’s left-hand rule}}$$

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: One parsec in astronomical units (A.U.) is about
solution:
We know:

  • $1 \, \text{parsec} = 3.26 \, \text{light years}$

  • $1 \, \text{light year} \approx 6.3 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.}$

So,

$$ 1 \, \text{parsec} = 3.26 \times 6.3 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.} $$

$$ \approx 20.5 \times 10^4 \, \text{A.U.} $$

$$ = 2.05 \times 10^5 \, \text{A.U.} $$

So, one parsec is about:

$$ 2 \times 10^5 \, \text{A.U.} $$

Correct option: Option 3

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: Which one of the following statements for second law of motion is NOT correct ?
options: The net force on a body is proportional to that body’s acceleration
The net force on a body is in the same direction as the acceleration
The time rate of change of momentum is equal to force
The net force on a body is proportional to that body’s momentum
solution:
The statement which is NOT correct is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 4: The net force on a body is proportional to that body’s momentum}}$$

Step-by-step:

According to Newton’s Second Law of Motion:

$$F = \frac{dp}{dt}$$

This means:

  • Force is equal to the time rate of change of momentum

  • If mass is constant, then:

$$F = ma$$

So:

  1. Option 1 is correct because force is proportional to acceleration.

  2. Option 2 is correct because acceleration is in the same direction as net force.

  3. Option 3 is correct because it is the actual statement of the second law.

  4. Option 4 is incorrect because force is not proportional to momentum, but to the rate of change of momentum.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 4}}$$

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: The temperature of a body increases  from 310 K to 340 K. The temperature increase in degree Celsius is
solution:
Temperature difference in kelvin and degree Celsius is the same.

Given:

$$ T_1 = 310\ \text{K}, \quad T_2 = 340\ \text{K} $$

Increase in temperature:

$$ \Delta T = T_2 - T_1 = 340 - 310 = 30\ \text{K} $$

So, the increase in degree Celsius is also:

$$ 30^\circ \text{C} $$

Correct option: 2. 30°C

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: Which one of the following statements for the work done by gravity on a body is NOT correct ?
options: Work done is independent of the path followed by the body
Work done depends only on the vertical distance separating the initial and the final position of the body
Work done depends on the path followed by the body
Work done is zero if the body is displaced horizontally to the force of gravity
solution:
The incorrect statement is:

Option 3: Work done depends on the path followed by the body

Step-by-step explanation

Gravity is a conservative force. For a conservative force:

  • Work done does not depend on the path

  • It depends only on the initial and final positions

For gravity, work done is given by:

$$ W = mg(h_1 - h_2) $$

where:

  • $m$ = mass of the body

  • $g$ = acceleration due to gravity

  • $h_1$ = initial height

  • $h_2$ = final height

So:

  • If only the vertical height changes, gravity does work.

  • If the displacement is purely horizontal, then there is no change in height, so work done by gravity is zero.

Checking each option

  1. Work done is independent of the path followed by the body

    This is correct.

  2. Work done depends only on the vertical distance separating the initial and the final position of the body

    This is correct.

  3. Work done depends on the path followed by the body

    This is not correct.

  4. Work done is zero if the body is displaced horizontally to the force of gravity

    This is correct.

Final answer

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 3}} $$

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: Which one among the following statements is correct ?
options: Pressure has magnitude as well as direction:
As we go deeper in a liquid, the pressure decreases
Action and reaction forces act on the same object
Pascal and N/m<sup>2</sup> represent the same unit
solution:
The correct statement is:

$$\text{Option 4: Pascal and } N/m^2 \text{ represent the same unit}$$

Now let us check each option one by one:

  1. Pressure has magnitude as well as direction

    This is wrong because pressure is a scalar quantity.

    It has only magnitude, not direction.

  2. As we go deeper in a liquid, the pressure decreases

    This is wrong.

    In a liquid, pressure increases with depth.

  3. Action and reaction forces act on the same object

    This is wrong.

    According to Newton’s third law, action and reaction act on different objects.

  4. Pascal and $N/m^2$ represent the same unit

    This is correct because

$$1 \, \text{pascal} = 1 \, N/m^2$$

So, the correct answer is Option 4.

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: Which one of the following wavelength radiations may correspond to an X-ray ?
solution:
X-rays have very short wavelengths, usually in the range of about

$$ 0.01 \text{ nm to } 10 \text{ nm} $$

Now check the options:

  • $1 \text{ nm}$: lies in the X-ray range

  • $10 \text{ nm}$: at the extreme end, but usually not taken as the standard answer here

  • $100 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

  • $300 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{1 \text{ nm}} $$

Option 1.

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: In a Telescope, compared to the eyepiece lens the objective lens has
options: larger focal length and larger aperture
larger focal length and smaller aperture
smaller focal length and larger aperture
smaller focal length and smaller aperture
solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 1: larger focal length and larger aperture}$$

Step-by-step:

  1. In a telescope, the objective lens is the lens facing the distant object.

  2. Its job is to collect as much light as possible from faraway objects.

  3. So, it must have a large aperture.

Also:

  1. The objective lens forms a real, inverted, and diminished image of the distant object.

  2. For better magnification in a telescope, the objective lens should have a large focal length.

  3. The eyepiece acts like a magnifying glass, so it has a small focal length.

Therefore, compared to the eyepiece, the objective lens has:

  • larger focal length

  • larger aperture

Hence, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1}}$$

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: The energy, E, of a photon can be expressed as E = hf where f is the frequency and h is Planck’s constant. The dimensions of h are the same as that of
solution:
Given,

$$ E = hf $$

We need the dimensions of $h$.

Step 1: Write dimensions of energy

Energy has the dimensions:

$$ [E] = ML^2T^{-2} $$

Step 2: Write dimensions of frequency

Frequency is the number of oscillations per second, so:

$$ [f] = T^{-1} $$

Step 3: Find dimensions of $h$

From

$$ h = \frac{E}{f} $$

So,

$$ [h] = \frac{ML^2T^{-2}}{T^{-1}} = ML^2T^{-1} $$

Step 4: Compare with given options

  • Linear momentum:

$$ p = mv $$

Dimensions:

$$ [p] = MLT^{-1} $$

Not same.

  • Angular momentum:

$$ L = mvr $$

Dimensions:

$$ [L] = MLT^{-1} \cdot L = ML^2T^{-1} $$

Same.

  • Displacement:

$$ [L] $$

Not same.

  • Torque:

$$ \tau = \text{force} \times \text{distance} $$

Dimensions:

$$ [\tau] = (MLT^{-2})(L) = ML^2T^{-2} $$

Not same.

Correct option:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 2: angular momentum}} $$

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: The work done by the force acting on an object is zero if the displacement of the object
options: is in the opposite direction of the direction of force
is in the same direction of the direction of force
is in perpendicular direction of the direction of force
solution:
Work done is given by

$$ W = F s \cos \theta $$

where:

  • $F$ = force

  • $s$ = displacement

  • $\theta$ = angle between force and displacement

For work done to be zero,

$$ \cos \theta = 0 $$

This happens when

$$ \theta = 90^\circ $$

That means the displacement is perpendicular to the direction of force.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 3: is in perpendicular direction of the direction of force

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: The magnetic field inside a current carrying very long solenoid is
solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 1: Uniform

For a very long current-carrying solenoid, the magnetic field inside is nearly the same at all points inside it.

According to NCERT, the magnetic field lines inside a long solenoid are:

  • parallel

  • equally spaced

  • directed along the axis of the solenoid

This shows that the magnetic field inside is uniform.

So, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Uniform}}$$

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: Which one of the following instruments can be used to measure —250°C temperature ?
options: By using a mercury based thermometer
By using an alcohol based thermometer
solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 4: By using thermocouple based thermometer}$$

Step by step:

  1. $-250^\circ \text{C}$ is an extremely low temperature.

  2. A mercury thermometer cannot be used because mercury freezes at about

$$-39^\circ \text{C}$$

  1. An alcohol thermometer can measure lower temperatures than mercury, but not as low as $-250^\circ \text{C}$.

  2. A clinical thermometer is used only for measuring human body temperature, usually in the range

$$35^\circ \text{C} \text{ to } 42^\circ \text{C}$$

  1. A thermocouple thermometer is used for very high as well as very low temperatures.

Therefore, to measure $-250^\circ \text{C}$, we use

$$\boxed{\text{thermocouple based thermometer}}$$

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: The quantity of heat needed to change unit mass of a substance from liquid to vapour without changing the temperature, is called
solution:
The correct answer is Option 2: specific latent heat.

Explanation:

The quantity of heat needed to change unit mass of a substance from liquid to vapour without any change in temperature is called specific latent heat of vaporisation.

In NCERT terms:

  • Latent heat: Heat absorbed or released during change of state.

  • Specific latent heat: Heat required for unit mass of a substance to change its state without change in temperature.

So here, since the change is from liquid to vapour, it is specific latent heat.

Therefore,

$$ \text{Correct option} = \text{Option 2} $$

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: Which one of the following statements about surface tension is correct ?
options: It will remain same with increase or decrease of temperature
It will always decrease with increase or decrease of temperature
solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 2: It decreases when temperature increases.

Explanation:

Surface tension is the property of a liquid surface due to which it behaves like a stretched membrane.

When temperature increases, the molecules of the liquid gain more kinetic energy and move more freely. Because of this, the intermolecular force of attraction becomes less effective at the surface, so the surface tension decreases.

Therefore:

$$ \text{Surface tension decreases with increase in temperature} $$

So, the correct option is Option 2.

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: A body sinks in water when
options: density of water is greater than density of body
density of water is less than density of body
density of water is equal to density of body
solution:
A body sinks in water when its density is greater than the density of water.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 2: density of water is less than density of body

Reason:

According to the NCERT concept of flotation:

  • If density of body $>$ density of liquid, the body sinks

  • If density of body $<$ density of liquid, the body floats

  • If density of body $=$ density of liquid, the body may remain suspended

Here, for sinking in water:

$$ \text{Density of body} > \text{Density of water} $$

which means

$$ \text{Density of water} < \text{Density of body} $$

Hence, Option 2 is correct.

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: Why a fielder in the ground gradually pulls his hands backward while catching a fast moving cricket ball ?
options: To reduce the acceleration of the ball
To increase the acceleration of the ball
To increase the velocity of the ball
solution:
The correct answer is Option 1: To reduce the acceleration of the ball.

Explanation:

When a fielder catches a fast-moving cricket ball, the ball has a large momentum. To stop it, the fielder has to bring its momentum to zero.

We know:

$$ \text{Force} = \frac{\Delta p}{\Delta t} $$

where:

  • $\Delta p$ = change in momentum

  • $\Delta t$ = time taken

The change in momentum is fixed because the ball has to be stopped. So, if the fielder pulls his hands backward, the time of stopping $\Delta t$ increases.

When time increases, the force decreases.

Also, since acceleration is related to force by

$$ F = ma $$

less force means less acceleration (actually less retardation).

So, by moving his hands backward, the fielder reduces the acceleration of the ball, and therefore the force on his hands becomes less. This prevents injury.

Final Answer:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 1: To reduce the acceleration of the ball}} $$

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: Bottom of a tank containing water appears to be raised. It is due to
solution:
It is due to Option 2: Refraction of light.

Explanation:

When light rays come from the bottom of the tank and pass from water to air, they bend at the surface because of refraction.

Since the rays bend away from the normal on coming from a denser medium (water) to a rarer medium (air), the bottom appears to be at a smaller depth than its real depth.

So, the bottom of the tank appears raised.

Correct answer:

$$\text{Refraction of light}$$

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: SONAR stands for
solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 3: Sound Navigation and Ranging

SONAR is a technique that uses sound waves to detect and locate objects under water.

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: Which one of the following radiations has longer wavelength ?
solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 3: Micro Wave

Reason:

In the electromagnetic spectrum, wavelength increases in this order:

$$ \text{X-rays} < \text{Ultraviolet} < \text{Infra-red} < \text{Microwaves} $$

So, among the given options, microwaves have the longest wavelength.

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: For a material under static conditions, which one among the following statements is correct ?
options: Both perfect conductor and insulator can be charged
Both perfect conductor and insulator cannot be charged
solution:
Static conditions mean that electric charges are not moving. They stay still.

In an insulator, charges cannot move freely. They stay in one place where they are put.

This means an insulator can keep an electric charge when conditions are static.

In a conductor, charges can move around easily. This makes it hard to keep the charges in one spot.

⇒ Under static conditions, an insulator can be charged.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1.

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: A person can see nearby objects clearly but cannot see distant object distinctly, the person is suffering with
solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 3: Myopia}$$

Step by step:

  • In myopia (short-sightedness), a person can see nearby objects clearly.

  • But distant objects appear blurred.

So the given condition matches myopia.

Final answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Myopia}}$$

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: The energy is always conserved for a system which is
solution:
Energy stays the same, or is "conserved," in a system when it doesn’t exchange energy or matter with anything outside of it.

An "isolated system" is a system where this no-exchange condition is met.

In an isolated system, the total energy doesn’t change over time, which means energy conservation is always present.

For systems that are "non-isolated," energy can move in and out, so energy conservation isn't guaranteed for just that system.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1: Isolated only.

title: NDA General Ability 14th September 2025
question: Suppose, a ball of mass M is thrown upwards from a point A and it reaches up to the highest point B and returns back to point A, which one among the following is correct ?
options: Kinetic Energy at A = Potential Energy at B
Kinetic Energy at A = Potential Energy at A
Kinetic Energy at B = Potential Energy at B
Kinetic Energy at B = Kinetic Energy at A
solution:
The correct option is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1: Kinetic Energy at A = Potential Energy at B}}$$

Step-by-step explanation:

Let the ball be thrown upward from point $A$ with initial speed $u$.

At point $A$:

  • The ball has kinetic energy

$$KE_A = \frac{1}{2}Mu^2$$

  • If we take point $A$ as the reference level, then

$$PE_A = 0$$

At highest point $B$:

  • The velocity becomes zero, so

$$KE_B = 0$$

  • Let the height reached be $h$, then potential energy at $B$ is

$$PE_B = Mgh$$

Now, by conservation of mechanical energy:

$$\frac{1}{2}Mu^2 = Mgh$$

So,

$$KE_A = PE_B$$

Hence, Option 1 is correct.

Why other options are wrong:

  • Option 2: At point $A$, potential energy is usually taken as zero, so it is not equal to kinetic energy.

  • Option 3: At point $B$, kinetic energy is zero but potential energy is not zero.

  • Option 4: At point $B$, kinetic energy is zero, but at $A$ it is maximum.

$$\boxed{\text{Correct Answer: Option 1}}$$

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: Two identical spring balances S1 and S2 are connected one after the other and are held vertically as shown in the figure. A mass of 10 kg is hanging from S2. If the readings on S1 and S2 are W1 and W2 respectively, then :
solution:
Step 1:

Let the hanging mass be $m = 10 \,\text{kg}$.

The weight of the mass is

$$ W = mg = 10 \times 9.8 = 98 \,\text{N}.$$

For convenience, in such problems, instead of Newton, we express the balance reading directly in “kg‑weight.”

So, the force corresponds to 10 kg‑weight.

Step 2:

Spring balance S₂ is directly attached to the mass.

Therefore, S₂ supports the whole 10 kg mass.

So,

$$ W_2 = 10 \,\text{kg}. $$

Step 3:

Now look at spring balance S₁.

It is supporting both S₂ and the 10 kg mass hanging below it.

Thus, S₁ also bears the same total weight of $10 \,\text{kg}$.

So,

$$ W_1 = 10 \,\text{kg}. $$

Final Answer:

$$ W_1 = 10 \,\text{kg}, \quad W_2 = 10 \,\text{kg} $$

Correct Option: D ✅

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: A stone is thrown horizontally from the top of a 20 m high building with a speed of 12 m/s. It hits the ground at a distance R from the building. Taking g = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup> and neglecting air resistance will give :
solution:
1. Vertical motion

  • Initial vertical velocity $u_y = 0$

  • Height $h = 20\,$m

  • Acceleration $g = 10\,$m/s²

  • Use $$h = \tfrac12\,g\,t^2$$

    so $$20 = \tfrac12\cdot10\cdot t^2 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad t^2 = 4 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad t = 2\ \text{s}$$

  1. Horizontal motion

  • Horizontal speed $v_x = 12\,$m/s (constant)

  • Range $R = v_x \,t = 12 \times 2 = 24\,$m

Answer: R = 24 m (Option C)

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: A sphere of volume V is made of a material with lower density than water. While on Earth, it floats on water with its volume f<sub>1</sub>V (f<sub>1</sub> < 1) submerged. On the other hand, on a spaceship accelerating with acceleration a < g (g is the acceleration due to gravity on Earth) in outer space, its submerged volume in water is f<sub>2</sub>V. Then:
options: f2 = (1 - $\frac{a}{g}$) f1​
f2 > f1​
f2 = $\frac{a}{g}$ f1​
solution:
1. Let the density of the sphere be ρ_s and that of water be ρ_w. Its mass is

    $$m = \rho_s V\,. $$

  2. On Earth (acceleration = g):

    Equilibrium ⇒ weight = buoyant force

    $$m g = (\text{mass of displaced water})\;g = \bigl(\rho_w f_1 V\bigr)\,g\,. $$

    ⇒ $$\rho_s V g = \rho_w f_1 V g \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f_1 = \frac{\rho_s}{\rho_w}\,. $$

  3. In the spaceship accelerating at a (< g):

    Effective “gravity” = a for both sphere and water.

    Equilibrium ⇒ $m\,a =$ buoyant force = $(\rho_w f_2 V)\,a$.

    $$\rho_s V a = \rho_w f_2 V a \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f_2 = \frac{\rho_s}{\rho_w}\,. $$

  4. Hence

    $$f_2 = f_1\,. $$

Answer: Option A.

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: Two identical containers X and Y are connected at the bottom by a thin tube of negligible volume. The tube has a valve in it, as shown in the figure. Initially container X has a liquid filled up to height h in it and container Y is empty. When the valve is opened, both containers have equal amount of liquid in equilibrium. If the initial (before the valve is opened) potential energy of the liquid is P1 and the final potential energy is P2 then :
options: P1 = 4P2
P1 = 2P2
P1 = 8P2
solution:
1. Let the cross-sectional area of each container be $A$ and the liquid density be $\rho$. Total mass of liquid initially in $X$ is

    $$m = \rho A h.$$

  2. Choose the bottom of the containers as the reference level ($y=0$).

    – Initial centre of mass of liquid in $X$ is at height $h/2$.

    – Initial potential energy

    $$P_1 = m g \,\frac{h}{2} = (\rho A h)\,g\,\frac{h}{2} = \frac{\rho g A h^2}{2}.$$

  3. After opening the valve, the liquid distributes equally: each container has height $h/2$.

    – Mass in each container is $\rho A (h/2)$.

    – Centre of mass in each is at half that height, $\,\tfrac{h/2}{2}=h/4\,$.

    – Potential energy of liquid in one container:

    $$P_{\rm one} = \Bigl[\rho A \tfrac{h}{2}\Bigr] g \,\frac{h}{4} = \frac{\rho g A h^2}{8}.$$

    – Total final potential energy in both containers:

    $$P_2 = 2\,P_{\rm one} = 2\cdot\frac{\rho g A h^2}{8} = \frac{\rho g A h^2}{4}.$$

  4. Ratio

    $$\frac{P_1}{P_2} = \frac{\tfrac{\rho g A h^2}{2}}{\tfrac{\rho g A h^2}{4}} = \frac12\div\frac14 = 2 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad P_1 = 2\,P_2.$$

Answer: Option C.

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: A particle is moving in a circle of radius R with a constant speed v. Its average acceleration over the time when it moves over half the circle is :
solution:
1. Average acceleration definition

    $\displaystyle \vec a_{\text{avg}}=\frac{\Delta \vec v}{\Delta t}$

  2. Change in velocity over half circle

    – Initial speed $v$, final speed $v$, but directions opposite

    – $\displaystyle |\Delta \vec v|=|\vec v_f-\vec v_i|=2v$

  3. Time to move half the circle

    – Path length $=\pi R$, speed $=v$

    – $\displaystyle \Delta t=\frac{\pi R}{v}$

  4. Magnitude of average acceleration

    $$a_{\text{avg}}=\frac{|\Delta \vec v|}{\Delta t} =\frac{2v}{\pi R/v} =\frac{2v^2}{\pi R}$$

Answer: $\displaystyle \frac{2v^2}{\pi R}$ (Option C)

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: Two forces of 5⋅0 N each are acting on a point mass. If the angle between the forces is 60°, then the net force acting on the point mass has magnitude close to :
solution:
1. Given two forces each of magnitude $5.0\,$N, with angle between them $\theta=60^\circ$.

  2. By the law of cosines, the resultant $R$ satisfies

    $$R^2 = F_1^2 + F_2^2 + 2\,F_1\,F_2\cos\theta.$$

  3. Substitute $F_1=F_2=5.0\,$N and $\cos60^\circ=\tfrac12$:

    $$R^2 = 5^2 + 5^2 + 2\cdot5\cdot5\cdot\tfrac12 = 25 + 25 + 25 = 75.$$

  4. Therefore

    $$R = \sqrt{75} = 5\sqrt3 \approx 5\times1.732 = 8.66\ {\rm N}.$$

  5. Closest value: 8.6 N.

Answer: Option A (8.6 N).

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: A rectangle ABCD is kept in front of a concave mirror of focal length f with its corners A and B being, respectively, at distances 2f and 3f from the mirror with AB along the principal axis as shown in the figure. It forms an image A'B'C'D' in front of the mirror. What is the ratio of B'C' to A'D'?
solution:
The Mirror Equation:

When rays emanating from a point meet at another point after reflection or refraction, that point is termed the image of the initial point.

The image is termed real if the rays actually converge at that point.

It is virtual if the rays do not actually meet but appear to diverge from that point when extended backward.

The mirror equation is given by: $\frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{u}$

Linear magnification (m) is defined as the ratio of the height of the image ($h'$) to the height of the object ($h$): $m = \frac{h'}{h} = -\frac{v}{u}$

Calculation:

Given rectangle ABCD with AD = BC ---- (1)

Corners A and B are at distances 2f and 3f from the mirror, respectively.

For corner A (u = 2f): $ \frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{2f} \implies v = 2f $

Magnification: $ m = \frac{h'}{h} = -\frac{v}{u} = -\frac{2f}{2f} = -1 $ This means the size of the image is equal to the size of the object: $ AD = A'D' ---- (2) $

For corner B (u = 3f): $ \frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{3f} \implies v = \frac{3}{2}f $

Magnification: $ m = \frac{h'}{h} = -\frac{v}{u} = -\frac{\frac{3}{2}f}{3f} = -\frac{1}{2} $ This means the size of the image is half the size of the object: $ B'C' = \frac{1}{2}BC ---- (3) $

From equations 1, 2, and 3, the ratio of B'C' to A'D' is: $ \frac{B'C'}{A'D'} = \frac{\frac{1}{2}BC}{AD} = \frac{1}{2} $

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: Shown in the figure are two plane mirrors XY and YZ (XY ⊥ YZ) joined at their edge. Also shown is a light ray falling on one of the mirrors and reflected back parallel to its original path as a result of this arrangement. The two mirrors are now rotated by an angle θ to their new position X'YZ', as shown. As a result the new reflected ray is at an angle α from the original reflected ray. Then :
solution:
Explanation:

When two plane mirrors are at right angles (90°), a light ray falling on one mirror reflects back in exactly the opposite direction.

This makes the angle between the incoming ray and the outgoing ray equal to 180°.

In the figure, mirrors XY and YZ meet at a right angle.

Now the pair of mirrors is rotated by an angle θ to the new position X'YZ'.

After this rotation, the reflected ray shifts by an angle α compared to the original reflected ray.

However, the reflected ray remains parallel to its original direction, so α = 0°.

Therefore, the new reflected ray is parallel to the original reflected ray.

Additional Information

If two mirrors are placed at an angle other than 90°, the incoming ray and the reflected ray are no longer exactly opposite.

In that case, the angles of the reflected rays depend on the angle between the mirrors and on the incidence and reflection angles at each mirror.

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: A railway wagon (open at the top) of mass M<sub>1</sub> is moving with speed v<sub>1</sub> along a straight track. As a result of rain, after some time it gets partially filled with water so that the mass of the wagon becomes M<sub>2</sub> and speed becomes v<sub>2</sub>. Taking the rain to be falling vertically and the water stationery inside the wagon, the relation between the two speeds v<sub>1</sub> and v<sub>2</sub> is :
options: $\frac{1}{2}$M1$v^2_1$ < $\frac{1}{2}$ M2$v^2_2$​
M1v1 = M2v2
M1v1 < M2v2
solution:
Let's analyze the problem. Since the rain is falling vertically and the water inside the wagon is stationary, the horizontal momentum of the system (wagon + water) is conserved.

Conservation of Momentum

The initial horizontal momentum of the system is given by: $$ M_1 v_1 $$

The final horizontal momentum of the system, after the wagon has collected water, is: $$ M_2 v_2 $$

According to the principle of conservation of momentum: $$ M_1 v_1 = M_2 v_2 $$

Therefore, the correct relation between the two speeds $ v_1 $ and $ v_2 $ is given by Option C: $$ M_1 v_1 = M_2 v_2 $$

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: Which one of the following statements is not true for a flute, a musical instrument?
options: Momentum of waves on the blowing jet determines the loudness of the produced note.
Arrival time of the waves on the blowing jet determines the pitch of the produced note.
Sound comes from a vibrating column of air inside the flute.
Sound comes from a vibrating column of air inside as well as outside the flute.
solution:
The statement that is not true for a flute is:

Option D:

Sound comes from a vibrating column of air inside as well as outside the flute.

Flutes produce sound primarily from the vibrating column of air inside the instrument. The resonance and pitch are determined by the length of this air column and the placement of the finger holes. While external waves can influence the characteristics of the sound, the primary source is the internal air column.

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: A positive charge is moving towards south in a space where magnetic field is pointing in the north direction. The moving charge will experience :
options: a deflecting force towards north direction.
a deflecting force towards east direction.
a deflecting force towards west direction.
solution:
The correct answer is Option D (no deflecting force). Here's why:

Understanding the Concept:

Magnetic Force on a Moving Charge: A charged particle moving in a magnetic field experiences a force only if its velocity has a component perpendicular to the magnetic field direction.

Right-Hand Rule: You can determine the direction of the magnetic force using the right-hand rule. However, in this scenario, we don't need to apply the rule.

Explanation:

Since the positive charge is moving parallel (towards South) to the magnetic field (pointing North), there is no perpendicular component of velocity.

In simpler terms, the charge is moving "along" the magnetic field lines, not "across" them. Therefore, it experiences no magnetic force to deflect it.

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: Which one of the following astronomers proved that the Earth and other planets revolve around the Sun?
solution:
The correct answer is:

Nicolaus Copernicus was the astronomer who formulated a model of the universe that placed the Sun rather than the Earth at its center, a concept known as heliocentrism. This model was revolutionary and paved the way for future astronomers to further develop and confirm the idea that the Earth and other planets revolve around the Sun.

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: Sand falls vertically on a conveyor belt at a work rate of 0⋅1 kg/s. In order to keep the belt moving at a uniform speed of 2 m/s, the force required to be applied on the belt is :
solution:
To determine the force required to keep the conveyor belt moving at a uniform speed while sand falls vertically onto it, you can use the relationship between force, mass flow rate, and velocity. Here are the steps to solve this problem:

Mass flow rate of sand:
$ \dot{m} = 0.1 \, \text{kg/s} $

Velocity of the belt:
$ v = 2 \, \text{m/s} $

Momentum change per second (force required):
The force exerted by the falling sand on the belt is equal to the rate of change of momentum of the sand. Since the sand is falling vertically and starts moving horizontally with the belt upon landing, the horizontal force required can be calculated as:

$ F = \dot{m} \cdot v $

Substituting the given values:

$ F = 0.1 \, \text{kg/s} \cdot 2 \, \text{m/s} $

$ F = 0.2 \, \text{N} $

So, the force required to keep the belt moving at a uniform speed of 2 m/s is:

Option B: 0.2 N

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: The power required to lift a mass of 8⋅0 kg up a vertical distance of 4 m in 2 s is (taking acceleration due to gravity as 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>):
solution:
To determine the power required, we need to follow these steps:

Calculate the work done to lift the mass.

Calculate the power using the time in which the work is done.

Step 1: Calculate the Work Done

Work done $ W $ is given by:

$ W = m \cdot g \cdot h $

where:

$ m $ is the mass ($ 8.0 \, \text{kg} $),

$ g $ is the acceleration due to gravity ($ 10 \, \text{m/s}^2 $),

$ h $ is the height ($ 4 \, \text{m} $).

So,

$ W = 8.0 \, \text{kg} \times 10 \, \text{m/s}^2 \times 4 \, \text{m} $

$ W = 320 \, \text{J} $

Step 2: Calculate the Power

Power $ P $ is given by:

$ P = \frac{W}{t} $

where $ t $ is the time ($ 2 \, \text{s} $).

So,

$ P = \frac{320 \, \text{J}}{2 \, \text{s}} $

$ P = 160 \, \text{W} $

Conclusion

The power required to lift the mass is $ 160 \, \text{W} $. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option B: 160 W

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: One block of 2⋅0 kg mass is placed on top of another block of 3⋅0 kg mass. The coefficient of static friction between the two blocks is 0⋅2. The bottom block is pulled with a horizontal force F such that both the blocks move together without slipping. Taking acceleration due to gravity as 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>, the maximum value of the frictional force is :
solution:
To determine the maximum value of the frictional force that allows the two blocks to move together without slipping, we need to consider the forces acting between the two blocks.

The maximum static frictional force $$ (f_{s,\text{max}}) $$ can be calculated using the formula:

$$ f_{s,\text{max}} = \mu_s \cdot N $$

Where:

  • $$ \mu_s $$ is the coefficient of static friction, which is 0.2 in this case.
  • $$ N $$ is the normal force exerted by the top block on the bottom block.

The normal force $$ (N) $$ is equal to the weight of the top block. We can calculate this as follows:

$$ N = m \cdot g $$

Where:

  • $$ m $$ is the mass of the top block, which is 2.0 kg.
  • $$ g $$ is the acceleration due to gravity, which is 10 m/s2.

Substituting the values, we get:

$$ N = 2.0 \, \text{kg} \times 10 \, \text{m/s}^2 = 20 \, \text{N} $$

Now, we can find the maximum static frictional force:

$$ f_{s,\text{max}} = 0.2 \times 20 \, \text{N} = 4 \, \text{N} $$

Thus, the maximum value of the frictional force is:

Option C: 4 N

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: A mass is attached to a spring that hangs vertically. The extension produced in the spring is 6 cm on Earth. The acceleration due to gravity on the surface of the Moon is one-sixth of its value on the surface of the Earth. The extension of the spring on the Moon would be:
solution:
A spring that obeys Hooke's Law extends by a distance proportional to the force applied to it. When a mass $ m $ is attached to a vertical spring, the force applied to the spring is the weight of the mass, which can be expressed as $ F = mg $, where $ g $ is the acceleration due to gravity.

Given that the extension produced in the spring on Earth is 6 cm (or 0.06 m), we can write this as:

$$ F_\text{Earth} = k \cdot \Delta x_\text{Earth} $$

where $ k $ is the spring constant and $ \Delta x_\text{Earth} $ is the extension on Earth. Rearranging this equation, we get:

$$ mg = k \cdot 0.06 $$

Since we are comparing the extension on the Moon to the Earth, we need to consider the change in the gravitational force. On the Moon, the acceleration due to gravity $ g_\text{Moon} $ is one-sixth of that on Earth:

$$ g_\text{Moon} = \frac{g}{6} $$

The force exerted by the mass on the Moon would then be:

$$ F_\text{Moon} = mg_\text{Moon} = m \cdot \frac{g}{6} $$

This force causes a different extension in the spring, which we will denote as $ \Delta x_\text{Moon} $. Applying Hooke’s Law on the Moon, we have:

$$ F_\text{Moon} = k \cdot \Delta x_\text{Moon} $$

Substituting for $ F_\text{Moon} $ from earlier, we get:

$$ m \cdot \frac{g}{6} = k \cdot \Delta x_\text{Moon} $$

Since we know from the Earth’s situation that $ mg = k \cdot 0.06 $, we can substitute $ mg $ into the equation:

$$ \frac{k \cdot 0.06}{6} = k \cdot \Delta x_\text{Moon} $$

Simplifying this, we find:

$$ \Delta x_\text{Moon} = \frac{0.06}{6} = 0.01 \, \text{m} $$

Therefore, the extension of the spring on the Moon would be 1 cm. So, the correct answer is:

Option B: 1 cm

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: Which one of the following is an example of Second Class Lever?
solution:
To determine which of these options exemplifies a Second Class Lever, we need to first understand the characteristics of the three types of levers.

A lever is defined by its three components: the effort, the load, and the fulcrum. These are arranged differently in each class:

First Class Lever: The fulcrum is situated between the effort and the load. An example is a seesaw.

Second Class Lever: The load is located between the effort and the fulcrum. An example is a wheelbarrow.

Third Class Lever: The effort is applied between the fulcrum and the load. An example is a pair of tweezers.

Now, let's examine each option:

Option A: A pair of scissors operates as a First Class Lever because the fulcrum is between the effort applied by the hand and the load exerted by the blades on the paper.

Option B: A bottle opener functions as a Second Class Lever. Here, the fulcrum is at one end (where the opener pivots on the bottle cap), the effort is applied at the handle, and the load (the resistance of the bottle cap) sits in the middle.

Option C: A cricket bat is an example of a Third Class Lever. The effort (force of the player's hands) is applied between the fulcrum (where the handle is gripped) and the load (the ball hitting the bat).

Option D: A bow and arrow do not conform to the typical lever classifications as they primarily involve the concepts of tension and elasticity, rather than the mechanical advantage given by levers.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B: A bottle opener

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: In an electric circuit, a wire of resistance 10 Ω is used. If this wire is stretched to a length double of its original value, the current in the circuit would become :
options: four times of its original value.
solution:
In order to determine how the current in the circuit would change when the wire's length is doubled, we need to understand the relationship between resistance, length, and cross-sectional area of a wire.

The resistance ($ R $) of a wire is given by the formula:

$$ R = \rho \frac{L}{A} $$

where:

  • $ \rho $ is the resistivity of the material
  • $ L $ is the length of the wire
  • $ A $ is the cross-sectional area of the wire

If the wire is stretched to double its original length, the new length ($ L' $) is:

$$ L' = 2L $$

Because the volume of the wire remains constant when it is stretched, the cross-sectional area ($ A $) will be reduced. The original volume ($ V $) is:

$$ V = A \cdot L $$

After stretching, the new volume ($ V' $) is:

$$ V' = A' \cdot L' $$

$$ V' = A' \cdot 2L $$

$$ V = V' $$

Thus:

$$ A \cdot L = A' \cdot 2L $$

$$ A' = \frac{A}{2} $$

Therefore, the new cross-sectional area is half of the original area. Now, the new resistance ($ R' $) can be calculated using the new length and cross-sectional area:

$$ R' = \rho \frac{L'}{A'} $$

$$ R' = \rho \frac{2L}{\frac{A}{2}} $$

$$ R' = \rho \frac{2L}{A} \cdot 2 $$

$$ R' = 4 \cdot \rho \frac{L}{A} $$

$$ R' = 4R $$

So the new resistance is four times the original resistance. Since current ($ I $) is inversely proportional to resistance for a constant voltage ($ V $), we have:

$$ I = \frac{V}{R} $$

$$ I' = \frac{V}{R'} $$

$$ I' = \frac{V}{4R} $$

$$ I' = \frac{I}{4} $$

Hence, the current in the circuit would become one-fourth of its original value when the wire is stretched to double its length.

The correct option is: Option C: one-fourth of its original value.

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: What is the total resistance in the following circuit element?
solution:
To find the total resistance in the given circuit, we first need to understand the configuration of the resistors. The image shows three resistors arranged in a combination of series and parallel.

Let's denote the resistances as follows:

  • Two resistors in series with each resistance $$R$$
  • One resistor in parallel with the series combination mentioned above with resistance $$R$$

First, we calculate the equivalent resistance of the two resistors in series:

$$R_{series} = R + R = 2R$$

Next, we consider the parallel combination of this series resistance with the third resistor:

The total resistance for resistors in parallel, where one resistor has resistance $$R$$ and the other has resistance $$2R$$, is given by the formula:

$$\frac{1}{R_{total}} = \frac{1}{R} + \frac{1}{2R}$$

Combining the two fractions, we get:

$$\frac{1}{R_{total}} = \frac{2}{2R} + \frac{1}{2R} = \frac{3}{2R}$$

Taking the reciprocal to find the total resistance:

$$R_{total} = \frac{2R}{3}$$

Therefore, the total resistance in the circuit is $$\frac{2R}{3}$$.

So the correct option is:

Option D $$\frac{2R}{3}$$

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: Which one of the following statements is true?
options: The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon is greater than the force of gravity of the Moon on the Earth.
The force of gravity of the Moon on the Earth is greater than the force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon.
The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon and of the Moon on the Earth are equal in magnitude and are in the same direction.
The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon and of the Moon on the Earth are equal in magnitude but are in opposite directions.
solution:
To determine the correct answer, we need to apply Newton's Third Law of Motion. Newton's Third Law states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. This means the forces two objects exert on each other are equal in magnitude but opposite in direction.

In the case of the Earth and the Moon, the gravitational force that the Earth exerts on the Moon is equal in magnitude to the gravitational force that the Moon exerts on the Earth. However, these forces are in opposite directions.

Mathematically, the force due to gravity between two masses, $m_1$ (Earth) and $m_2$ (Moon), is given by Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation:

$$ F = G \frac{m_1 m_2}{r^2} $$

where $G$ is the gravitational constant, and $r$ is the distance between the centers of the two masses.

Since the masses and the distance between the centers remain the same, the magnitude of the force is the same for both the Earth and the Moon. Hence, by Newton's Third Law, we know that these forces are also equal in magnitude and opposite in direction.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option D: The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon and of the Moon on the Earth are equal in magnitude but are in opposite directions.

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: An electric bulb is rated as 220 V and 80 W. When it is operated on 110 V, the power rating would be:
solution:
To determine the power rating of the electric bulb when operated at a different voltage, let's start by using the given ratings. The bulb is rated as 220 V and 80 W. First, we can calculate the resistance of the bulb using the power formula:

$$ P = \frac{V^2}{R} $$

Given:

$$ P = 80 \, \text{W} $$

$$ V = 220 \, \text{V} $$

Rearranging the formula to solve for resistance \( R \):

$$ R = \frac{V^2}{P} $$

Substituting the values:

$$ R = \frac{220^2}{80} $$

Calculating the resistance:

$$ R = \frac{48400}{80} = 605 \, \Omega $$

Now, we will find the power consumed when the bulb is operated at 110 V. Using the power formula again:

$$ P' = \frac{V'^2}{R} $$

Where:

$$ V' = 110 \, \text{V} $$

$$ R = 605 \, \Omega $$

Substituting these values into the formula:

$$ P' = \frac{110^2}{605} $$

Calculating the power:

$$ P' = \frac{12100}{605} \approx 20 \, \text{W} $$

So, when the bulb is operated at 110 V, the power rating would be approximately 20 W. The correct answer is:

Option D: 20 W

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: In the dispersion of white light by a common glass prism, which one among the following is correct?
options: Red light deviates the most because red light has highest speed in prism
Blue light deviates the most because blue light has highest speed in prism
Red light deviates the most because red light has lowest speed in prism
Blue light deviates the most because blue light has lowest speed in prism
solution:
In the dispersion of white light by a common glass prism, the different colors of light have different speeds when traveling through the prism. The speed of light in a medium is inversely related to the refractive index of that medium for that particular color. As a result, light with a shorter wavelength (like blue light) slows down more and, therefore, bends more than light with a longer wavelength (like red light) when it enters and exits the prism.

When white light passes through a prism, it is dispersed into its constituent colors due to the difference in the refractive indices for different wavelengths. Blue light has a shorter wavelength compared to red light, which means it has a higher refractive index in the glass prism. Consequently, blue light deviates or bends more than red light.

So, the correct answer is:

Option D: Blue light deviates the most because blue light has the lowest speed in the prism.

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: Which one among the following is true for the speed of sound in a given medium?
options: Speed of sound remains same at all frequencies
Speed of sound is faster at higher frequencies
Speed of sound is slower at higher frequencies
Speed of sound is slower at higher wavelengths
solution:
The speed of sound in a given medium depends primarily on the properties of the medium, such as its density and elasticity, rather than the frequency of the sound. Let's analyze each option:

Option A: Speed of sound remains same at all frequencies

This option is generally true for a given medium. The speed of sound in a particular medium remains largely constant regardless of the frequency of the sound wave. The formula for the speed of sound in a medium is given by:

$$ v = \sqrt{\frac{B}{\rho}} $$

where $ v $ is the speed of sound, $ B $ is the bulk modulus (or stiffness) of the medium, and $ \rho $ is the density of the medium. Since this formula does not involve frequency, the speed remains the same for different frequencies in the same medium.

Option B: Speed of sound is faster at higher frequencies

This option is incorrect. There is no direct correlation between higher frequencies and faster speeds in a given medium based on the principle stated above.

Option C: Speed of sound is slower at higher frequencies

This option is also incorrect. There is no direct correlation between higher frequencies and slower speeds in a given medium.

Option D: Speed of sound is slower at higher wavelengths

This option is misleading. The relationship between wavelength ($ \lambda $) and frequency ($ f $) is given by:

$$ v = f \lambda $$

Given that the speed of sound $ v $ remains constant in a medium, if the wavelength increases, the frequency decreases, and vice versa. However, this does not imply that the speed is slower at higher wavelengths because the speed $ v $ itself is constant for that medium.

Therefore, Option A is the most accurate statement: the speed of sound remains the same at all frequencies in a given medium.

title: NDA General Ability 16 April 2023
question: Which one of the following telescopes contains only mirrors?
solution:
The correct answer is:

Option C: Newtonian telescope

A Newtonian telescope is a type of reflecting telescope invented by Sir Isaac Newton. It uses a concave primary mirror to gather light and reflect it to a flat secondary mirror, which then reflects the light to an eyepiece positioned on the side of the telescope. This design does not include any lenses, relying solely on mirrors for its operation.

Here are brief descriptions of the other options for comparison:

Option A: Galilean telescope

A Galilean telescope is a type of refracting telescope named after Galileo Galilei. It uses a convex objective lens and a concave eyepiece lens. This design does not use mirrors but instead two lenses to focus light and form an image.

Option B: Keplerian telescope

A Keplerian telescope, also known as a Keplerian refractor, is similar to the Galilean telescope but uses two convex lenses. The objective lens gathers light and the eyepiece lens magnifies the image. Like the Galilean telescope, it uses lenses rather than mirrors.

Option D: Schmidt telescope

A Schmidt telescope, also known as a Schmidt camera, uses a combination of a spherical primary mirror and a corrector plate (lens) to correct spherical aberrations. Although it primarily relies on a mirror, it also incorporates a lens, therefore it does not consist entirely of mirrors.

Thus, the Newtonian telescope is the only type among the given options that uses only mirrors.

title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
question:
The electrostatic potential doesn't depend on which of the following 

(1) It depends on the charge

(2) It depends on the endpoints of the path through work done

(3) It depends on the path taken for the work done 

(4) It depends on the amount of work done
solution:
The electrostatic potential does not depend on the path taken for the work done. This is because the electrostatic force is a conservative force, which means that the work done by or against an electrostatic force in moving a charge between two points depends only on the initial and final positions of the charge and is independent of the path taken.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: Only statement 3

title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
question: Mirage is an illustration of
options: only total internal reflection of light.
both refraction and total internal reflection of light.
solution:
A mirage is an illustration of both refraction and total internal reflection of light.

Explanation:

Mirages occur when layers of air at different temperatures cause light to bend. This is primarily due to refraction, which is the bending of light as it passes from one medium to another with a different density.

In the case of a hot road, the air near the surface is significantly warmer than the air above it. Light from the sky bends as it moves through these layers of varying density, and in some cases, this bending can continue until the angle of incidence exceeds the critical angle for total internal reflection.

Key Concepts:

Refraction: Occurs when light waves pass from one medium to another and change speed, causing the light to bend. This bending creates the initial path alteration of light.

Total Internal Reflection: After sufficient bending, if light strikes the boundary at a specific angle (greater than the critical angle), it reflects entirely back into the medium instead of passing through.

Together, refraction and total internal reflection help explain why a mirage makes a distant object, like the sky, appear as though it is reflecting off the ground, creating the illusion of water. Hence, Option D - both refraction and total internal reflection of light - is correct.

title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
question:
Consider the following statements about Light year:

1. Light year is a unit for measurement of very large distances.

2. Light year is a unit for measurement of very large time intervals.

3. Light year is a unit for measurement of intensity of light.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
solution:
Only statement 1 is correct.

A light year is a unit of distance, not time or intensity of light. It is defined as the distance that light travels in a vacuum in one year. The speed of light is approximately $299,792,458$ meters per second, so over the course of a year, this distance is about:

$$ 1 \text{ light year} = 9.461 \times 10^{15} \text{ meters} $$

Thus, the correct option is D: 1 only.

title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
question: Which one of the following regarding density of water at atmospheric pressure is correct?
options: Density of water at 4°C is 1000 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
Density of water at 0°C is 1000 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
Density of water at 0°C is 100 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
Density of water at 4°C is 10 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
solution:
Option A is correct.

The density of water at 4°C is approximately 1000 kg/m³. At this temperature, water is at its maximum density. This value is derived from the definition of the kilogram, which was historically based on the mass of 1 liter of water at its maximum density point, or 4°C.

Water exhibits anomalous expansion, meaning that it reaches its maximum density not at the freezing point (0°C) but slightly above it at 4°C. Below 4°C, water begins to expand as it approaches the freezing point. This is one reason why ice floats on water—because its density is less than that of liquid water.

The density of water decreases as the temperature increases beyond 4°C due to thermal expansion. Therefore, the understanding of water's density at various temperatures is crucial for many scientific and engineering applications.

title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
question:
Which of the following pairs of physical phenomenon and the discover is/are correctly matched?

 1. James Chadwick  Photoelectric
 2. Albert Einstein  Neutron
 3. Marie Curie  Radium
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
solution:
The correct matching pair is "Marie Curie and Radium." Marie Curie, along with her husband Pierre Curie, discovered the element Radium. The other pairs are mismatched:

James Chadwick is known for discovering the neutron, not for the photoelectric effect.

Albert Einstein is recognized for his explanation of the photoelectric effect, not for discovering the neutron.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option D: 3 only.

title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
question: The statement "friction force is a contact force while magnetic force is a non-contact force" is
options: either true or false depending upon the temperature of the surroundings.
solution:
The statement "friction force is a contact force while magnetic force is a non-contact force" is always true, so the correct option is Option A.

Friction force requires two surfaces in contact with each other to resist the relative motion between them. This interaction is a contact force because it necessitates physical contact between objects.

Magnetic force, on the other hand, acts at a distance. Magnets can exert forces on other magnets or magnetic materials without requiring physical contact between them. Since magnetic forces can influence objects separated by air or vacuum, they are considered non-contact forces.

Temperature does not change the fundamental nature of these forces. Friction will always need contact, and magnetism will always act at a distance, irrespective of temperature conditions.

title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
question: The composition of gases in exosphere is
solution:
The exosphere is primarily composed of light gases. Helium and hydrogen are the two main constituents found in this outermost layer of Earth's atmosphere.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A: Helium and Hydrogen.

title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
question: Which one of the following is the lowest possible temperature?
solution:
The lowest possible temperature among the given options is Option D: $-273$° Celsius. This temperature is equivalent to absolute zero, which is 0 Kelvin ($0 \, \text{K}$). Absolute zero is the theoretical temperature at which substances contain no thermal energy and particles are at their minimum motion. It marks the complete absence of heat energy.

In the Celsius scale, absolute zero is expressed as $-273.15$° Celsius. Thus, any temperature lower numerically like $-273$° Celsius, symbolically represents this concept of absolute zero. No temperature can be lower than absolute zero as it is the absolute limit of thermal motion in particle physics.

Therefore, in the context of the given options, $-273$° Celsius is the representation of the theoretical minimum temperature possible.

title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
question: Numerically two thermometers, one in Fahrenheit scale and another in Celsius scale shall read same at
solution:
The temperatures on the Fahrenheit scale ($F$) and the Celsius scale ($C$) are related by the equation:

$$ F = \frac{9}{5}C + 32 $$

To find the temperature at which both thermometers read the same, we need to solve for the temperature when $F = C$. Substituting $C$ for $F$ in the equation gives:

$$ C = \frac{9}{5}C + 32 $$

Subtract $\frac{9}{5}C$ from both sides:

$$ C - \frac{9}{5}C = 32 $$

This simplifies to:

$$ -\frac{4}{5}C = 32 $$

Multiply both sides by $-\frac{5}{4}$ to solve for $C$:

$$ C = 32 \times -\frac{5}{4} $$

$$ C = -40 $$

Therefore, the correct option is Option A: -40°.

At -40 degrees, both the Fahrenheit and Celsius scales read the same.

title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
question: The image we see in plane mirror is
options: real and thus can be photographed.
virtual and nearer than the object.
solution:
The image observed in a plane mirror is described as virtual and is laterally inverted. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option C

Virtual and is laterally inverted.

Explanation:

Virtual Image: A virtual image is formed when the outgoing rays from a point of the object appear to diverge. In the case of a plane mirror, the image cannot be projected onto a screen because the light rays do not actually converge at the image location; they only appear to do so when seen by the observer.

Lateral Inversion: This term refers to the horizontal reversal of the image. For example, if the left hand of a person is raised while standing in front of the mirror, it appears that the right hand is raised in the mirror image.

Virtual images cannot be captured directly on a screen but can be seen by looking into the mirror. It is important to note that a photograph of the mirror itself capturing the virtual image can be taken, but it would not be a direct image of the virtual image.

title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
question: Point of Origin of Earthquake Wave is known as
solution:
The point of origin of an earthquake wave is known as the Focus (Option B).

In seismology, the focus of an earthquake refers to the location within the Earth where the rupture of the fault begins, and seismic waves are initially generated. It is beneath the surface of the Earth. The point directly above the focus, on the Earth's surface, is called the epicenter.

Focus: The exact point inside the Earth where an earthquake originates.

Epicenter: The point on the Earth's surface located directly above the focus.

These concepts are fundamental in understanding how energy is released during an earthquake and how it propagates through the Earth's crust.

title: NDA General Ability 18 April 2021
question: Which one of the following conclusions could not be derived from Rutherford's α-particle scattering experiment?
options: Most of the space in the atom is empty
The radius of the atom is about 10<sup>5</sup> times the radius of the nucleus
Electrons move in a circular path of fixed energy called orbits
Nearby all the mass of the atom resides in the nucleus
solution:
